name: Blokus Trigon
author: Bernard Tavitian (Sekkoia 2006; Mattel) / AGP
description: Blokus on a triangular lattice: a hexagonal board of 486 triangles, and four players each holding the same 22 pieces — every free polyiamond of 1 to 6 triangles (1 + 1 + 1 + 3 + 4 + 12; rotations and reflections both allowed). Six starting points are SHARED, so any colour may open on any of them; your first piece must cover one. Every later piece must touch one of your own pieces at a corner and must never share an edge with your own colour, while pieces of different colours may touch freely. A blocked player passes; the game ends when all four are blocked. Score -1 per remaining unit triangle, +15 for placing all 22 pieces, +5 more if your last piece was the single triangle. Highest score wins.
